category_title: Preschoolers with autism exhibiting high unengagement and rigid object manipulation
minimal_context: The student is sitting at a table during structured centers working on completing an animal puzzle.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time for structured centers. Here is the animal puzzle. Please put the pieces in the board.

STUDENT: (Picks up an animal puzzle piece and stares at it, remaining entirely object-focused without looking at the teacher.)

TUTOR: Keep trying. Put the piece in.

STUDENT: (Starts moving the animal puzzle piece across the table as if it were walking and making corresponding animal sounds.)

TUTOR: You need to finish completing your puzzle.

STUDENT: (Ignores the puzzle board and continues to manipulate the toy in a rigid, stereotyped manner, slipping from the puzzle activity into a state of unengagement with the teacher.)

options.A: Treat play and joint attention as separate skill domains to be specifically taught and reinforced.
options.B: Verbally reiterate and review all rules, supported with picture symbols, daily before structured centers.
options.C: Deliver twelve hour-long sessions, scheduled twice-weekly, of a mindfulness intervention program for the student.
options.D: Fade the level of assistance over time so that it becomes more feasible and ecologically appropriate.
